title: limonium vulgare subsp. pseudolimonium
intro: Limonium vulgare subsp. pseudolimonium, also known as sea lavender, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Plumbaginaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of sea lavender include reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and calming digestive issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions.
